# Restore Requirements Checklist: Partial Backup Restore

**Purpose**: Validate completeness, clarity, consistency, and coverage of the partial-restore requirements before implementation and PR review
**Created**: 2026-05-27
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the exact backup formats eligible for partial restore fully enumerated, including which encrypted and compressed variants are supported versus full-restore-only? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-020, Spec Supported Backup Eligibility, Contract: Partial-Restore Eligibility Contract]
- [x] CHK002 Are the exact tables in the required identity/auth restore group explicitly specified in the requirements instead of only described as "related identity/auth tables"? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-021, Data Model: RequiredRestoreGroup, Contract: Selection Rules]
- [x] CHK003 Are requirements defined for non-identity dependency groups beyond the `users` group, including whether they must be auto-included or blocked when selected indirectly? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-025, Spec FR-026, Spec: Dependency And Conflict Policy]
- [x] CHK004 Are requirements complete for how unsupported, malformed, or uninspectable backups are identified and communicated before the workflow falls back to full restore? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-008, Spec FR-014, Contract: Partial-Restore Eligibility Contract, Contract: `POST /admin/backup/partial-restore/inspect`]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Is "minimum dependent records required to keep those tables usable" defined precisely enough to distinguish automatic inclusion from validation failure for each dependency class? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-025, Spec FR-026, Spec: Dependency And Conflict Policy]
- [x] CHK006 Is "conflicting existing rows" defined clearly enough for tables with composite primary keys, surrogate keys, or no detectable primary key? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-027, Data Model: RestoreConflictSummary, Spec: Dependency And Conflict Policy]
- [x] CHK007 Is "supported backup source" quantified well enough to determine eligibility rules separately for SQLite database files and PostgreSQL plain dumps? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-020, Spec Supported Backup Eligibility, Contract: Partial-Restore Eligibility Contract]
- [x] CHK008 Is the requirement for "strong warnings" on sensitive tables specific enough to define what the warning must contain and when it must appear? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-016, Spec FR-018, Contract: Selection Rules]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK009 Are the requirements consistent about whether partial restore operates at table level only, without any row-level selection or row-diff review in v1? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec Assumptions, Plan Summary, Research: Execute partial restore in the Flask admin app with backend-specific adapters]
- [x] CHK010 Are the requirements consistent about full restore remaining available as a fallback rather than being replaced or merged into the same execution path? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-008, Spec Assumptions, Research: Preserve full restore as the fallback path for unsupported or uninspectable backups]
- [x] CHK011 Are the terms "selected tables," "required dependent data," and "required restore groups" used consistently enough to avoid conflicting interpretations between the spec, plan, and data model? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec: Selected Restore Scope, Spec: Dependency And Conflict Policy, Data Model: RestorableTable, Data Model: RequiredRestoreGroup]
- [x] CHK012 Do the sensitivity requirements align consistently between table selection defaults, warning behavior, and audit visibility for identity-related restores? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-010, Spec FR-016, Spec FR-018, Security/Audit/Operations, Contract: Audit Event Contract]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK013 Can SC-001 be measured objectively with a defined representative backup size, supported backend, and starting point in the admin workflow? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec: Validation Baseline, Spec SC-001]
- [x] CHK014 Can SC-002 be objectively verified without ambiguity about what counts as an "unrelated current record" or how changes are detected? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec: Validation Baseline, Spec SC-002]
- [x] CHK015 Can SC-004 be measured consistently without a defined denominator for "supported partial restore attempts" and what counts as requiring fallback full restore? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec: Validation Baseline, Spec SC-004]
- [x] CHK016 Are outcome-summary requirements specific enough to determine whether counts, statuses, warnings, and blocked reasons must all appear in the final results view? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-011, Spec SC-005, Data Model: TableRestoreResult, Contract: `GET /admin/backup/partial-restore/results/<run_id>`]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK017 Are requirements defined for the full multi-step admin flow, including inspect, select, preview, execute, and results review, rather than only the selection and execution endpoints? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec User Story 1, Spec User Story 2, Spec User Story 3, Contract: Admin Partial Restore HTTP Workflow]
- [x] CHK018 Are requirements complete for retry and repeat-run scenarios, including what should happen when the same partial restore is attempted twice after skipped conflicts? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-028, Spec: Dependency And Conflict Policy, Research: Skip conflicting existing rows by default and import only non-conflicting rows]
- [x] CHK019 Are requirements defined for stale preview scenarios, such as current database changes occurring between preview and execution? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-023, Data Model: PartialRestoreRun, Contract: `POST /admin/backup/partial-restore/execute`, Contract: Preview and Execution State Rules]
- [x] CHK020 Are requirements complete for backend-specific inspection failures, including parse errors for PostgreSQL dumps and unreadable SQLite backups? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec Edge Cases, Contract: Partial-Restore Eligibility Contract, Contract: `POST /admin/backup/partial-restore/inspect`]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK021 Are requirements defined for tables that exist in the backup but not in the current schema, or in the current schema but not in the backup? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-029, Spec Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK022 Are requirements complete for selected tables whose dependency targets are partially present but not fully restorable, especially across identity/auth tables and cross-module foreign keys? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-026, Spec FR-029, Spec: Dependency And Conflict Policy]
- [x] CHK023 Are requirements defined for concurrent admin partial-restore attempts against the same environment, including blocking, queuing, or race-avoidance expectations? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-024, Plan Operations Impact, Contract: `POST /admin/backup/partial-restore/execute`, Contract: Preview and Execution State Rules]
- [x] CHK024 Are requirements complete for temporary inspection and preview artifacts, including how long they may persist and when they must be deleted after success, failure, or abandonment? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-023, Plan Data/Migration Impact, Contract: Preview and Execution State Rules]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK025 Are audit requirements specific enough to require logging of selected tables, required restore groups, skipped conflict counts, warnings, and final per-table outcomes? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-010, Contract: Audit Event Contract]
- [x] CHK026 Are security requirements complete for decrypting backups during inspection, especially around key-source precedence, failed decryption behavior, and visibility of sensitive table metadata before restore? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-009, Security/Audit/Operations, Contract: `POST /admin/backup/partial-restore/inspect`]
- [x] CHK027 Are localization requirements defined for backend-specific parse failures, sensitive-table warnings, conflict summaries, and result-state labels across all steps of the workflow? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-030, Spec Localization Impact, Plan Localization Impact]
- [x] CHK028 Are performance expectations defined for inspection and preview generation on large backups, or intentionally left out of scope with an explicit operational assumption? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec: Validation Baseline, Plan Performance Goals]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK029 Are the assumptions about temporary filesystem-backed preview state documented with enough precision to determine whether persistence, cleanup, and crash recovery requirements are missing? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Plan Data/Migration Impact, Contract: Preview and Execution State Rules]
- [x] CHK030 Are dependencies on current backup generation behavior documented clearly enough to know whether future backup-format changes would invalidate partial-restore eligibility? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-031, Spec Assumptions, Plan Operations Impact]
- [x] CHK031 Are the requirements explicit about which existing environment settings and operational docs govern partial restore, rather than assuming operators will infer them from full-restore behavior? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec Deployment/Configuration Impact, Plan Operations Impact, Contract: Operational References]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK032 Is there a conflict between allowing "any backed-up table" to be selected and blocking unsupported or unsafe partial-restore content, or are the boundaries between selectable and actually executable tables fully defined? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-008, Spec FR-015, Contract: Partial-Restore Eligibility Contract, Contract: Selection Rules]
- [x] CHK033 Is it clear whether row counts shown in preview must be exact or may be estimates when the backup backend or parser limits precision? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Data Model: RestorableTable, Data Model: RestoreConflictSummary, Contract: `POST /admin/backup/partial-restore/inspect`, Contract: `POST /admin/backup/partial-restore/preview`]
- [x] CHK034 Does the spec define whether blocked tables may still appear in the final execution scope summary when other selected tables succeed, or is that outcome ambiguous? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-006, Spec FR-011, Contract: `POST /admin/backup/partial-restore/execute`, Contract: `GET /admin/backup/partial-restore/results/<run_id>`]
- [x] CHK035 Are the requirements explicit about whether SCIM tokens, audit logs, and other security-adjacent tables are part of the identity-sensitive selection model or intentionally excluded from the required `users` restore group? [Resolved 2026-05-27, Spec FR-022, Contract: Selection Rules]

## Notes

- Checklist profile: `PR reviewer`, `Standard` depth.
- Focus areas applied: data integrity and dependencies, security and sensitive tables, admin UX and localization, operations and fallback behavior.
- Updated on 2026-05-27 after contract and spec remediation review; unresolved items are the ones that still need spec, plan, or data-model clarification rather than checklist-only status changes.
